info.name: text-delete
info.text: Just like plants, ideas take nurturing in order to grow, while a neglected idea will wither.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Ideas are like plants because what you end up with is a bit unpredictable and not always what you imagined.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Ideas are like plants because you have to keep pruning away the unwanted offshoots and bad parts in order to have a successful outcome.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Ideas are like plants because some are tame and orderly, like trimmed hedges, and some grow wild and free like climbing vines and wildflowers.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Ideas are like plants because they both require hope; without hope no one would bother to plant a seed or try to come up with an idea whose outcome is unknown.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Ideas are like plants because the way they turn out depends on the environment; ideas that are encouraged thrive just as plants that are grown in the right zone thrive.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: You put in the work, and you get the reward.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Ideas are like plants because the end result reflects the effort put into them: you put in the work, and you get the reward.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Ideas are like plants because not everyone appreciates them; some people treat a new, challenging idea like an unwanted, invasive weed.

info.name: text-insert
info.text: They grow in the mind, and eventually take root.

info.name: text-delete
info.text: Ideas are like plants because the seed is first planted in the mind and only with time takes root outside the mind of the originator.
